reason: Round-1 challenge ch-bb571680 only PARTIALLY addressed. The revised inline derivation 'R(sx+ty)=lim_N R_N(sx+ty)=lim_N(s R_N x+t R_N y)=s Rx+t Ry by continuity of the vector-space operations' bundles TWO distinct sub-facts; only one is covered. (a) The +/scalar-mult continuity (last equality) follows from order-norm triangle inequality + homogeneity. (b) But the FIRST equality 'R(sx+ty)=lim_N R_N(sx+ty)' and the implicit 'R_N x->Rx, R_N y->Ry' are NOT continuity of vector operations: they convert node 1.2's OPERATOR-NORM convergence R_N->R into POINTWISE convergence at fixed vectors. That passage needs the operator-norm bound ||R_N(z)-R(z)|| <= ||R_N-R|| ||z||, established ONLY in node 1.1.2 (sup-definition of the induced operator norm). Node 1.6 neither cites node 1.1.2 nor declares it as a dependency (af deps 1.6 = 'No dependencies'; prose says only 'Uses node 1.2'). Node 1.2 establishes operator-norm convergence, NOT pointwise convergence at sx+ty. The phrase 'by continuity of the vector-space operations' silently papers over the operator-norm->pointwise step, an unprovenanced 'standard fact' the project rule forbids. Fix: cite node 1.1.2 for ||R_N(z)-R(z)|| <= ||R_N-R|| ||z|| and make R_N(z)->R(z) explicit before invoking +/scalar-mult continuity.
